title: Documentation, APIs, Licenses, and Generated-Artifact Gates · Signal Triage
summary: Keep human and machine contracts synchronized with implementation. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a documentation and contract drift suite and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Test README setup and core commands from a clean environment.
Validate API schemas, generated clients, migrations, and compatibility changes.
Test documentation links and executable examples where practical.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Generated Drift evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Generated Drift assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Use a changed debug case for gates docs contracts that tests this competency: Regenerate schemas, clients, docs, and fixtures and fail on unexplained diffs.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Generated Drift has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Generated Drift precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a gates-generated-drift-1gl0gr: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Generated Drift into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-generated-drift-1gl0gr:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Generated Drift structural check is only one gate. a documentation and contract drift suite must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: Name the Generated Drift precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write the debug evidence note for Generated Drift: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Use a changed debug case for gates docs contracts that tests this competency: Regenerate schemas, clients, docs, and fixtures and fail on unexplained diffs.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
steps.why: Retrieving Generated Drift through the concrete a documentation and contract drift suite case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Regenerate schemas, clients, docs, and fixtures and fail on unexplained diffs.
